summary: Large game-mode decomp/matching PR for src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c with companion header/type updates. It replaced many placeholder function stubs with C implementations, added local Classic-mode data-layout structs and STATIC_ASSERT size checks, updated prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where bodies exposed signatures, and produced measurable match progress while retaining many fuzzy matches plus one reported broken match attributed by the author to data/offset/linking work.

key_files.role: Primary implementation file; large replacement of placeholder decomp markers with C bodies, helper structs, data declarations, includes, and matching tactics.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists 5 new matches and 30 improved unmatched items in main/melee/gm/gm_1832

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for recovered gm_1832 functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes changed to typed signatures, e.g. fn_8018504C(void), fn_801851C0(void), fn_80185A0C(void), fn_80185E34(void), fn_80185F5C(s32), fn_80186634(void*), fn_80187910(HSD_GObj*)

key_files.role: Shared game-mode type additions supporting recovered bodies.
Cross-header signature update for a helper called from gm_1832.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80160DE8 changed from UNK_RET fn_80160DE8(UNK_PARAMS) to void fn_80160DE8(HSD_JObj*, u8, s32, u8, f32, f32)

what_changed.text: Converted a large set of gm_1832 placeholder functions into C implementations, including Classic-mode intro/splash/menu-related functions and OnEnter handlers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: many /// #fn_* markers replaced by function bodies in src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c
text_corpus: bot report lists new matches for fn_801861B8, fn_8018A364, fn_80188D3C, fn_8018504C, gm_80186E30_OnEnter

what_changed.text: Recovered file-local layout information with ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicCharData, ClassicModeEnterData, and related static assertions or offset comments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedefs and STATIC_ASSERTs added near lbl_804D6604 and Classic-mode enter-data code

what_changed.text: Expanded gm_1832.c includes to cover used baselib, game-mode, interface, item, language, and menu APIs.
Updated prototypes in gm_1832.h and gm_1601.h as implementations exposed concrete return and parameter types.
Produced progress but not full closure: report showed +1932 matched-code bytes, 5 new 100% matches, 30 improved unmatched items, one broken match, and one unmatched .bss regression.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report for GALE01 802c37b - 2f0587a

smart_moves.text: Used narrow local structs plus size checks to make large offset-based tables reviewable without pretending all semantic names were known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow with STATIC_ASSERT sizes and offset comments

smart_moves.text: Synchronized headers after recovering bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS surface instead of hiding signatures locally.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832.h and gm_1601.h signature updates

smart_moves.text: Author explicitly called out that many results were fuzzy and that a broken function was data/offset related, preserving uncertainty rather than overclaiming full matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'Lots of fuzzy matches, not a lot of 100% ones'
human_pr_text: 'The function that broke is data / offset stuff, will be resolved when linking'

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a large C replacement mainly improves fuzzy scores, keep the report ledger attached: distinguish new 100% matches from fuzzy improvements and record regressions separately.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report separates 5 new matches, 30 improvements, 1 broken match, and .bss regression
human_pr_text: author says there are many fuzzy matches and not many 100% ones

decomp_lessons.lesson: Typed interim structs with offsets and STATIC_ASSERTs are useful for turning raw data/offset regions into readable source while layout is still being recovered.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ClassicSlotVals/ClassicCharLayout/ClassicTeamEntry/ClassicSplashRow/ClassicModeEnterData added with offsets and size assertions

decomp_lessons.lesson: Function-body recovery should be accompanied by owning-header prototype cleanup when signatures become known.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1832.h and gm_1601.h prototypes changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures

naming_conventions.text: Kept many address-style symbol names for unresolved globals/functions while adding conservative role names for local helper structs such as ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicCharData, and ClassicModeEnterData.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: globals remain lbl_804735A8, lbl_804735E8, lbl_804D6604, lbl_803D9248 while new local typedefs use Classic* names

naming_conventions.text: Local variables use short role names where roles are evident, such as gobj, jobj, src, pos, scale, arrow, proc, player_slot.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8018325C and fn_80184138 locals include HSD_JObj* jobj/src/arrow and Vec3 pos/scale

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: PAD_STACK was used in at least fn_80184138 and fn_8018504C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence shows use of the tactic but not the exact objdiff justification for each instance.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Inline/no-inline steering appears around fn_801884F8/fn_8018846C call shaping, including an inline helper, a noInline wrapper, and pragmas.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pragma push/pop and dont_inline around nearby code
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as matching tactics requiring narrow verification; the slice does not include reviewer acceptance rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Data tables were represented through typed casts and file-local structs, e.g. ClassicCharData* data = (ClassicCharData*) lbl_803D9248.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static f32 lbl_803D9248[] and ClassicCharData cast in fn_801851C0
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is evidence of a data-layout tactic, not proof that final data ownership was resolved.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Repeated JObj transform code was expressed as loops over player/entity slots in recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) loops in fn_8018325C and fn_80184138

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code 59.50% (+0.05%, +1932 bytes), 5 new matches, 30 improvements in unmatched items, 1 broken match, and 1 unmatched .bss regression.
Author stated there were lots of fuzzy matches and not many 100% matches, but believed the PR was an improvement.
Author stated the broken function was data/offset related and would be resolved when linking.
No loaded review comments or formal review feedback are present in the slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep fuzzy-match progress separate from confirmed matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2329, the bot report separated 5 new 100% matches from 30 fuzzy improvements, while the author noted many fuzzy matches and a data/offset-related broken function. For similar PRs, preserve this distinction in summaries and do not overclaim fuzzy improvements as final matches.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use interim typed layout structs with size checks for offset-heavy game-mode data
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2329 made gm_1832 data accesses more reviewable by introducing file-local Classic* layout structs with offsets and STATIC_ASSERT size checks instead of leaving only broad padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ClassicSlotVals, ClassicCharLayout, ClassicTeamEntry, ClassicSplashRow, ClassicModeEnterData typedefs and assertions

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update owning headers when recovered bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2329 updated gm_1832.h and gm_1601.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures alongside the recovered C bodies.

source_update_candidates.title: gm_1832 is Classic/game-mode splash and menu matching work with offset-heavy data layouts
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2329 evidence shows src/melee/gm/gm_1832.c contains Classic-mode intro/splash/menu-related decomp work, with file-local Classic* layout structs and many address-style globals. Treat path work here as offset/data-layout sensitive and verify fuzzy progress carefully.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped caution for future gm_1832 curation; should be reviewed before adding to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Fuzzy progress PRs should preserve report categories and known regressions
source_update_candidates.text: Consider clarifying that PR summaries should distinguish new 100% matches, fuzzy improvements, broken matches, and data-section regressions when the author/report indicates partial progress.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with the existing verification-and-regression-ledger standard but PR #2329 provides a concrete example for curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not promote every function touched in the diff as matched; bot evidence only lists 5 new 100% matches and many fuzzy improvements.
Do not infer reviewer approval or requested changes; the loaded slice has no review comments.
Do not treat PAD_STACK, pragmas, inline/no-inline wrappers, or data casts as accepted general style from this PR alone; loaded evidence lacks per-tactic verifier rationale.
Do not claim the data/offset broken function was actually resolved by linking; only the author's expectation is present in the slice.
